name: peering
title: GCP Transit — VPC Network Peering
summary: Creates both sides of VPC network peering pairs with custom-route export/import control.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Retained for convention parity (network peerings are not labelable).

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code. Retained for convention parity (network peerings are not labelable).

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Retained for convention parity; VPC network peerings do not support labels.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: peerings
module.type: map(object({ network_a_self_link = string network_b_self_link = string a_to_b = optional(object({ export_custom_routes = optional(bool, false) import_custom_routes = optional(bool, false) export_subnet_routes_with_public_ip = optional(bool, true) import_subnet_routes_with_public_ip = optional(bool, false) }), {}) b_to_a = optional(object({ export_custom_routes = optional(bool, false) import_custom_routes = optional(bool, false) export_subnet_routes_with_public_ip = optional(bool, true) import_subnet_routes_with_public_ip = optional(bool, false) }), {}) }))
module.description: Map of VPC peering pairs keyed by logical name. GCP requires a peering resource on EACH network (this module creates both); the connection activates only when both exist. Subnet ranges must not overlap. export/import_custom_routes propagate static/dynamic routes (e.g., to share a hub's VPN routes with a spoke).

outputs.name: connection_ids
outputs.description: Map of '<peering-key>/a-to-b' and '<peering-key>/b-to-a' to peering resource IDs.

outputs.name: connection_states
outputs.description: Map of '<peering-key>/a-to-b' and '<peering-key>/b-to-a' to peering state (ACTIVE when both sides exist).
